R.M. Horton, H.D. Hunt, S.N. Ho, J.K. Pullen, L.R. Pease, Engineering hybrid genes without the use of restriction enzymes: gene splicing by overlap extension, Gene 77 (1989) 61-68.

nate i ce no agenti wo mieio! gether different regions of a gene in a one-tube PCR. In comparison with methods now available for assembling DNA segments of a gene in tandem, it is simple, convenient, and time-saving. As no nearby restriction sites are required or engineered into the mutagenic primer, a deletion can be introduced anywhere within the sequence of interest. If more than three DNA segments of a gene are to be assembled in tandem, one more round of PCR mutagenesis with the desired mutagenic primer should be performed using the mutant plasmid DNA obtained from the last round of PCR mutagenesis as template. We routinely use this procedure in our laboratory with great success.
